title: The woman who cleans my office at night
summary: Describe the night cleaner you rarely meet but whose careful work you notice every morning, and what you learned about her.
topic: The night cleaner whose work you only see in the morning
prompt: Write 9–11 sentences describing a person who does quiet, ordinary work that most people do not notice (a cleaner, a guard, a delivery rider). Describe what they do and what you learned about them. Use present simple for routines and past simple for the time you met.
hintStarters: I usually arrive at work before anyone else, so I often see…
Every morning the desks are clean and the bins are empty because…
One evening I stayed late and finally met…
She told me that she has three other buildings, and…

hintVocab.meaningVi: sạch bong, không một vết bẩn

sampleText: I work in a small office in Helsinki, and I usually arrive before anyone else. Every morning the desks are clean, the bins are empty, and the floor is spotless. For two years I never thought about who did this. The work was invisible to me, like it just happened by itself. Then one evening I stayed very late to finish a report. At nine o'clock a woman came in with a cart and a vacuum. Her name was Marta, and she works the night shift in three buildings, not only ours. She moves quietly so she does not disturb the few people who stay late. We talked for a few minutes while she emptied the bins. She told me she has done this job for eleven years and that she is proud when an office looks tidy in the morning. 'Nobody sees me,' she said, 'but they see my work.' I felt a little ashamed, because I had used the clean office every day and never said thank you. The next morning I left a short note on my desk for her. Now I always say good evening if I see her, and I keep my own desk less messy. Marta does ordinary work, but she does it carefully, and that is not a small thing.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I usually arrive before anyone else
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi thường đến trước tất cả mọi người

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The work was invisible to me
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: công việc đó là vô hình với tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: like it just happened by itself
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: như thể nó tự nhiên mà có

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a woman came in with a cart and a vacuum
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một người phụ nữ bước vào với một chiếc xe đẩy và máy hút bụi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: she works the night shift in three buildings
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bà làm ca đêm ở ba tòa nhà

sampleAnnotations.phrase: she does not disturb the few people who stay late
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bà không làm phiền vài người ở lại muộn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: she is proud when an office looks tidy
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bà tự hào khi một văn phòng trông gọn gàng
nhưng họ thấy công việc của tôi
tôi cảm thấy hơi xấu hổ
chưa bao giờ nói cảm ơn
tôi để lại một lời nhắn ngắn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I keep my own desk less messy
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi giữ bàn của mình bớt bừa bộn hơn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: that is not a small thing
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đó không phải là điều nhỏ nhặt

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for routine vs past simple for one event
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Bài dùng hiện tại đơn cho thói quen lặp lại: 'she works the night shift', 'she does not disturb'. Nhưng khi kể buổi tối gặp Marta, bài chuyển sang quá khứ đơn: 'a woman came in', 'we talked'. Hãy phân biệt việc lặp lại với một sự kiện đã xảy ra.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The text uses present simple for repeated routine: 'she works the night shift', 'she does not disturb'. But for the evening of meeting Marta it switches to past simple: 'a woman came in', 'we talked'. Separate the habit from the one finished event.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi làm việc trong một văn phòng nhỏ ở Helsinki, và tôi thường đến trước tất cả mọi người. Mỗi sáng bàn làm việc sạch sẽ, các thùng rác trống rỗng, và sàn nhà sạch bong. Suốt hai năm tôi chưa từng nghĩ đến ai đã làm việc này. Công việc đó là vô hình với tôi, như thể nó tự nhiên mà có. Rồi một buổi tối tôi ở lại rất muộn để hoàn thành một báo cáo. Lúc chín giờ một người phụ nữ bước vào với một chiếc xe đẩy và máy hút bụi. Tên bà là Marta, và bà làm ca đêm ở ba tòa nhà, không chỉ tòa nhà chúng tôi. Bà di chuyển nhẹ nhàng để không làm phiền vài người ở lại muộn. Chúng tôi nói chuyện vài phút trong khi bà đổ các thùng rác. Bà nói bà đã làm công việc này mười một năm và bà tự hào khi một văn phòng trông gọn gàng vào buổi sáng. 'Không ai thấy tôi,' bà nói, 'nhưng họ thấy công việc của tôi.' Tôi cảm thấy hơi xấu hổ, vì tôi đã dùng văn phòng sạch mỗi ngày mà chưa bao giờ nói cảm ơn. Sáng hôm sau tôi để lại một lời nhắn ngắn trên bàn cho bà. Giờ tôi luôn chào buổi tối nếu gặp bà, và tôi giữ bàn của mình bớt bừa bộn hơn. Marta làm công việc bình thường, nhưng bà làm nó cẩn thận, và đó không phải là điều nhỏ nhặt.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best-written sentence describing the clean office each morning.
options: Every morning the desks are clean and the floor is spotlessly.
Every morning the desks are cleaning and the floor is spotless.
Every morning the desks are clean and the floor is spotless.
Every morning the desks clean and the floor is spotless.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The floor is spotless' uses the adjective correctly. 'Spotlessly' is an adverb; 'are cleaning' is the wrong aspect; the last option drops 'are'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'the floor is', do you need an adjective or an adverb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that best describes her job in present simple.
options: She work the night shift in three buildings.
She is working the night shift in three buildings every life.
She works the night shift on three buildings.
She works the night shift in three buildings.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'She works' agrees with the subject and 'in three buildings' is natural. 'She work' lacks the -s; 'every life' is meaningless; 'on three buildings' is the wrong preposition.
mcQuestions.hint: Which form agrees with 'she' in present simple?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence narrates the evening correctly in past simple?
options: One evening I stayed late and met a woman with a cart.
One evening I stay late and met a woman with a cart.
One evening I stayed late and meet a woman with a cart.
One evening I have stayed late and met a woman with a cart.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs are past simple: 'stayed' and 'met'. The others mix tenses or use present perfect for a finished, specified time.
mcQuestions.hint: Are both actions finished events at a specific past time?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that says the work was unseen.
options: The work was invisible for me.
The work was invisible to me.
The work was unvisible to me.
The work was invisible at me.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Invisible to me' is the correct collocation. 'For me' and 'at me' are wrong prepositions; 'unvisible' is not a word.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sentence about her pride.
options: She is proud when an office looks tidily in the morning.
She is pride when an office looks tidy in the morning.
She is proud when an office looks tidy in the morning.
She is proud when an office look tidy in the morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Looks tidy' uses an adjective after 'look'. 'Tidily' is an adverb; 'pride' is a noun, not an adjective; 'look' must agree with 'an office'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence expresses regret about not thanking her?
options: I felt ashamed because I have never said thank you.
I felt ashamed because I never say thank you.
I felt ashamed because I had never say thank you.
I felt ashamed because I had never said thank you.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had never said' shows the missing thanks happened before the moment of shame. The others use the wrong tense or verb form.
mcQuestions.hint: Did the not-thanking happen before the feeling of shame?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about leaving a message.
options: The next morning I left a short note for her.
The next morning I leaved a short note for her.
The next morning I left a short note to her.
The next morning I left a short note her.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Left a note for her' is correct; 'left' is the past of 'leave'. 'Leaved' is wrong; 'to her' is less natural with 'note'; the last omits 'for'.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past tense of 'leave'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that uses 'disturb' naturally.
options: She moves quietly so she does not disturbs anyone.
She moves quietly so she does not disturb anyone.
She moves quietly so she not disturb anyone.
She moves quietly so she does not disturb to anyone.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'does not' use the base verb 'disturb' with no preposition. 'Disturbs' double-marks; 'not disturb' drops the auxiliary; 'to anyone' adds a wrong preposition.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'does not', is the verb base or -s form?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural way to value ordinary work?
options: She does ordinary work, but she does it careful.
She makes ordinary work, but she does it carefully.
She does ordinary work, but she does it carefully.
She does ordinary work, but she makes it carefully.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Does it carefully' uses the adverb and the right verb. 'Careful' is an adjective; 'makes work' is unnatural in English.
mcQuestions.hint: Do we 'do' work or 'make' work in English?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best sentence about no longer ignoring her.
options: Now I always say good evening if I will see her.
Now I always saying good evening if I see her.
Now I always say good evening if I saw her.
Now I always say good evening if I see her.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple in both clauses fits a repeated habit. 'Will see' after 'if' is wrong for habits; 'saying' lacks an auxiliary; 'saw' shifts to past.
mcQuestions.hint: For a present habit, what tense goes after 'if'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do we often value loud, visible work more than quiet work that we only notice when it stops? Is that fair?
